letterSpacing.$description: Primitive letter-spacing values for the River Romney design system. Negative values tighten tracking; positive values open it. ⚠️ Naming inconsistency: 'tight' (-0.025em) is actually more negative — and therefore tighter — than 'tightest' (-0.01em). These names should be swapped in a future refactor. Do not reference these tokens directly in UI code; use semantic tokens when available.

tightest.$description: Slightly reduced tracking. ⚠️ Despite the name, this is less tight than 'tight' (-0.025em) — naming should be corrected. Currently used for large display text where modest tracking reduction improves visual weight. Referenced by: unmapped.

tight.$description: Reduced tracking. ⚠️ Despite the name, this is actually tighter than 'tightest' (-0.01em) — naming should be corrected. Used for headings to tighten letter-spacing at medium display sizes. Referenced by: unmapped.

normal.$description: Default tracking. No adjustment to the typeface's built-in letter-spacing. The baseline for all body text and most UI text. Referenced by: unmapped.

wide.$description: Slightly open tracking. Used for UI labels and navigation where a small amount of open tracking improves readability at small sizes. Referenced by: unmapped.

wider.$description: Generously open tracking. For all-caps labels and display contexts where wide tracking is a deliberate typographic choice. Do not use at body sizes — wide tracking at 16px or below reads as broken text. Referenced by: unmapped.
